title: Boson Sampling with a reconfigurable 128 modes 3D integrated photonic circuit
authors: Simone Di Micco, Francesco Hoch, Alessandro Ciorra, Daniel Carvalho de Salles, Nicolò Spagnolo, Taira Giordani, Gonzalo Carvacho, Niki Di Giano, Marco Gardina, Andrea Crespi, Francesco Ceccarelli, Roberto Osellame, Fabio Sciarrino
abstract: Integrated quantum photonics has emerged as one of the leading platforms for scaling quantum information processing, offering compact, stable, and low-loss hardware with precise phase and mode control. Advances in integrated photonics architectures and active programmability now enable complex, high-dimensional transformations essential for quantum advantage tasks. We introduce an integrated, reconfigurable 3D photonic device with 128 modes for manipulation of single-photon quantum states (Qolossus 3D). Leveraging a continuously coupled architecture and thermo-optic programmability, the platform implements reconfigurable unitary transformations at unprecedented scale for integrated quantum optics. Exploiting indistinguishable single photons demultiplexed from a quantum dot source, we perform Boson Sampling across the large-dimensional chip and analyse the resulting output distributions for up to 4 photons. We then exploit it to demonstrate randomness generation via Boson Sampling. Agreement with theoretical predictions validates both the device's reconfigurable operation and the generation of random numbers. Our results highlight the scalability, stability, and precise control of integrated photonics for quantum information processing.
summary: The paper presents a new 128-mode 3D integrated photonic chip designed for large-scale quantum information processing. By implementing reconfigurable unitary transformations, the authors successfully performed Boson Sampling and demonstrated quantum-based randomness generation. This represents a significant step toward scalable, stable, and programmable photonic quantum computing.
main_problem: Scaling integrated photonic hardware to achieve high-dimensional unitary transformations required for quantum advantage tasks like Boson Sampling.
main_result: Demonstration of a reconfigurable 128-mode 3D integrated photonic circuit (Qolossus 3D) capable of performing Boson Sampling and generating randomness.
method: The researchers utilized a 3D integrated photonic device with a continuously coupled architecture and thermo-optic programmability, using single photons from a quantum dot source.
model_or_system: A 128-mode reconfigurable 3D integrated photonic circuit (Qolossus 3D) using a continuously coupled architecture and thermo-optic tuning.
key_observables: Output photon distributions for up to 4 photons and randomness generation metrics.
important_parameters: 128 modes, up to 4 photons, 3D integrated architecture.
main_assumptions: Indistinguishable single photons from a quantum dot source.
paper_structure: Introduction to integrated photonics, description of the Qolossus 3D architecture, implementation of Boson Sampling, demonstration of randomness generation, and validation against theoretical predictions.
why_interesting: This work is highly relevant for quantum optics and quantum information as it demonstrates a scalable hardware platform for high-dimensional quantum computing tasks and complex photonic state manipulation.
